title: [FIRRTL] Module ports get removed even when module is targeted by firrtl.simulation/firrtl.formal
body:
Consider this input FIRRTL:
```
FIRRTL version 6.0.0
circuit Foo :
  public module Foo :
    skip
  simulation GrayCodeExhaustive of GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
  module GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
    input clock : Clock
    input init : UInt<1>
    output done : UInt<1>
    output success : UInt<1>

    connect done, UInt<1>(0h1)
    connect success, UInt<1>(0h1)
```
When run through `firtool`, this produces the following error:
```
error: 'firrtl.simulation' op target @GrayCodeExhaustive_1 must have at least 4 ports, got 0 instead
  simulation GrayCodeExhaustive of GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
             ^
note: target defined here
  module GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
  ^
```
I think this is a bug in dead code / dead port elimination: if a module is targeted by a `firrtl.simulation` or a `firrtl.formal` declaration, we cannot change its port list, because those declarations rely on the module having specific ports.
code_blocks:
FIRRTL version 6.0.0
circuit Foo :
  public module Foo :
    skip
  simulation GrayCodeExhaustive of GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
  module GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
    input clock : Clock
    input init : UInt<1>
    output done : UInt<1>
    output success : UInt<1>

    connect done, UInt<1>(0h1)
    connect success, UInt<1>(0h1)
error: 'firrtl.simulation' op target @GrayCodeExhaustive_1 must have at least 4 ports, got 0 instead
  simulation GrayCodeExhaustive of GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
             ^
note: target defined here
  module GrayCodeExhaustive_1 :
  ^
